Yes, Phase 1 is implemented.

Why it matters: bridge-driven validator-join ingestion is now removed end-to-end, so validator admission is no longer routed through root-chain `Staked` listener/task plumbing.

What I changed:
1. Removed `Staked` event routing from listener switch in [rootchain_log.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/listener/rootchain_log.go:25).
2. Removed validator-join task registration from processor in [stake.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/processor/stake.go:74).
3. Removed the entire `sendValidatorJoinToGiltConsensus` processor path from [stake.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/processor/stake.go).
4. Updated affected tests in [rootchain_log_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/listener/rootchain_log_test.go:74) and [stake_test.go](/srv/repos/gold-chain/bridge/gilt-consensus/bridge/processor/stake_test.go:59).

Checks run:
- `go test ./bridge/listener -count=1` ✅
- `go test ./bridge/processor -count=1` ✅
- `go test ./x/stake/...` ✅
- `go test ./bridge/...` ❌

  Concrete fix path: shorten/alias those legacy amino message names (for `MsgUpdateParams` in affected modules like chainmanager/milestone) or stop registering those long names in legacy amino for tests that bootstrap full app.

What should be done next:
1. If you want full `go test ./bridge/...` green, I can implement the amino-name fix path next.
2. Otherwise, proceed to your Phase 2 scope.
